name: terraform-infrastructure
description: Terraform infrastructure as code workflow for provisioning cloud resources, creating reusable modules, and managing infrastructure at scale.
capabilities: Orchestrates Terraform project initialization with backend and provider configuration
Guides resource provisioning for compute, networking, and storage resources
Structures reusable Terraform module creation with variables and outputs
Configures remote state management with locking and workspaces
Sets up multi-environment deployments with isolation strategies
Integrates Terraform workflows with CI/CD pipelines including approval gates
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 31 potential security issues (27 external commands, 4 weak crypto). All findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The 'external commands' are markdown code references to other skills (e.g., `@terraform-skill`, `@terraform-specialist`) in documentation. The 'weak crypto' findings are documentation text mentioning cryptography, not actual implementation. This is a documentation-only workflow bundle with no executable code.
valueStatement: Managing complex Terraform infrastructure requires coordinating multiple skills and workflows. This skill bundle orchestrates Terraform setup, resource provisioning, module creation, and state management into a structured workflow that integrates with your CI/CD pipeline.
limitations: This is a workflow coordinator that references other skills which must be installed separately
Does not execute Terraform commands directly - requires manual invocation of suggested skills
Cloud provider specifics must be configured separately based on your target platform
State backend configuration varies by provider and requires manual setup

useCases.title: Cloud Infrastructure Setup
useCases.description: DevOps engineers provisioning new cloud environments use this workflow to structure Terraform projects from initialization through resource deployment.

useCases.title: Terraform Module Development
useCases.description: Platform teams creating reusable infrastructure components follow this workflow to build well-structured modules with proper interfaces.

useCases.title: Multi-Environment Deployment
useCases.description: Infrastructure leads managing production, staging, and development environments use this workflow to establish consistent multi-environment patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize Terraform Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a new Terraform project for deploying AWS resources. Configure the S3 backend for state storage and set up the AWS provider.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new infrastructure project from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Create Reusable Module
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to create a Terraform module for deploying a web application with load balancer, auto-scaling group, and security groups. Include input variables for customization.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building infrastructure components for reuse across teams

promptTemplates.title: Configure Multi-Environment
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up Terraform workspaces for dev, staging, and production environments. Each environment should have isolated state and variable files.
promptTemplates.scenario: Managing separate environments with consistent infrastructure

promptTemplates.title: Add CI/CD Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs terraform plan on pull requests and requires approval for terraform apply in the main branch.
promptTemplates.scenario: Automating infrastructure deployment with safety checks

bestPractices: Always run terraform plan before terraform apply to review changes before deployment
Use remote state backends with locking enabled to prevent concurrent state modifications
Separate state files per environment to maintain proper isolation and reduce blast radius
antiPatterns: Avoid storing sensitive values in Terraform state - use environment variables or secret managers
Do not manually modify cloud resources outside of Terraform as this creates configuration drift
Never commit .tfstate files to version control as they may contain sensitive data

faq.question: Does this skill execute Terraform commands directly?
faq.answer: No, this skill orchestrates workflows and suggests which specialized skills to invoke for specific Terraform tasks. You must have the referenced skills installed separately.

faq.question: Can I use this with any cloud provider?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow patterns apply to any cloud provider supported by Terraform including AWS, Azure, GCP, and others. Provider-specific configuration is handled by the specialized skills.

faq.question: How do I manage secrets in Terraform?
faq.answer: Never store secrets in Terraform code or state. Use environment variables, secret managers like AWS Secrets Manager, or integrate with your secrets-management skill for secure credential handling.

faq.question: What is the recommended backend for state storage?
faq.answer: Use cloud provider backends like AWS S3 with DynamoDB for locking, Azure Storage Account, or GCS. These provide durability, versioning, and locking mechanisms essential for team collaboration.

faq.question: How do I handle multiple environments?
faq.answer: The workflow recommends using Terraform workspaces or separate state files per environment with variable files for environment-specific configuration. This prevents cross-environment interference.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with existing CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow includes guidance for integrating with GitHub Actions and other CI/CD platforms. It sets up plan/apply stages with approval gates to ensure safe infrastructure changes.
